message:
feat(pthread/abi): expand futex-based condvar, TLS edge handling, RCU module, and pressure sensing

This commit addresses core threading primitives and ABI contracts that
underpin frankenlibc's POSIX compatibility layer:

- pthread condvar (cond.rs): Major expansion (~379 lines) to a futex-based
  condvar implementation. The previous spin-based approach suffered from
  priority inversion under contention and wasted CPU cycles in unikernel
  workloads where every cycle counts. The new implementation uses Linux
  futex semantics with proper wait queues, timeout handling, and
  broadcast/signal separation, matching musl's condvar contract.

- TLS (tls.rs): ~137 lines of improvements to thread-local storage
  handling, fixing edge cases around TLS destructors ordering and
  ensuring correct cleanup sequence on thread exit. This prevents
  use-after-free in TLS slots when destructors reference other TLS keys.

- RCU module (rcu/mod.rs): New 969-line read-copy-update implementation
  providing lock-free read-side critical sections. This is essential for
  the upcoming signal-safe data structure work and allows concurrent
  readers without blocking writers in the membrane layer.

- Pressure sensor (pressure_sensor.rs): New 613-line module in the
  membrane crate that monitors system resource pressure (memory, CPU,
  fd exhaustion) to enable adaptive backpressure in the harness.

- pthread ABI (pthread_abi.rs): 62-line expansion adding condvar-related
  ABI symbols (pthread_cond_init, _destroy, _wait, _timedwait, _signal,
  _broadcast) so the ABI crate's symbol table matches what the core
  crate now implements.

- startup ABI and contract tests: Updated to reflect the new entry
  points and validate ABI stability across versions.

- Bench and test additions: New condvar benchmark suite and pthread
  cond core contract tests to prevent regressions in the hot path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -44,3 +44,7 @@ harness = false

 name = "mutex_bench"

+[[bench]]
+name = "condvar_bench"
+harness = false
